dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower) to define range extremes. Entry triggers when price reaches or breaks above/below these extremes while in RANGING regime. Stop loss placed just below entry bar (1.5% conservative estimate). Exit when price reaches opposite extreme or breaks through range boundary. Signal is bidirectional—goes long at upper extreme, short at lower extreme.

backtest_rule.reason: Used Donchian channels (dc_upper, dc_lower) to define range extremes. Entry triggers when price reaches or breaks above/below these extremes while in RANGING regime. Stop loss placed just below entry bar (1.5% conservative estimate). Exit when price reaches opposite extreme or breaks through range boundary. Signal is bidirectional—goes long at upper extreme, short at lower extreme.

source_rule_text: In a ranging market, wait for price to reach the extremes of the range before entering; do not enter dips or rallies in the middle of the range.
